floor_history.headline: fable-blitz recon: timer/score packet builder (6 phases, ~144 insns) -- func_800600C8 (COMPLETED-C, same file) is a direct template: same S60C8 sprite-request struct, same func_8007352C chaining, same

frontier.hypothesis: Transplanting func_800600C8's S60C8 struct (extended with s16 d2 at +0x34 and u8 rgb bytes at +0x29..0x2B) plus its call/digit idioms reproduces phases 1-4 nearly line-for-line; the whole function drafts to a low masked distance in one pass.
frontier.mechanism: same compilation unit, same struct-on-stack address computation (GCC 2.7.2 reload of sp+0x18 base), same magic-div lowering (expand_divmod const path).
frontier.next_probe: Write the C body modeled on src/text1b.c:12969 (switch(s5) inside the 2-level digit loop, do-while trip counts 3/2/3x2/3/2), then sandbox func_8005D814 --disable all for the first measured floor.

frontier.hypothesis: Phase-4's d1 = %100 (0x51EB851F path) comes from source `(v/10)%100` or from writing digits as v/100, (v/10)%100, v%10 in that literal order -- NOT the sibling's hi%10 chain.
frontier.mechanism: GCC 2.7.2 expand_divmod emits the 0x51EB851F /100 magic for both /100 and %100 on s16; the observed dual mfhi interleave (mult t0 then a3 with same magic) implies two independent /100-class ops, no CSE.
frontier.next_probe: In the draft, A/B the two spellings ((v/10)%10 vs (v/10)%100) and diff the phase-4 window in the sandbox asm diff.

frontier.hypothesis: The three near-identical phase-3 case bodies are ONE source block under switch(field) (not unrolled copies): the per-iteration dispatch chain beq/slti/beqz proves live switch dispatch, so the C is a nested for with switch, and GCC's cross-jumping did NOT merge the tails (separate glyph constants 0x1A2/0x1D3/0x209 keep them apart).
frontier.mechanism: jump.c cross-jump pass only merges identical tails; distinct addiu constants block it, so per-case duplicated store+jump tails are expected from plain source-level switch.
frontier.next_probe: Draft with a single switch body; if tails wrongly merge or dispatch order flips, consult switch-vs-ifchain-branch-sense rule before restructuring.
